title: Gooey Green Spanish Pisto Paella
description: A rich, earthy, pillowy Spanish dish featuring panang-paste and kombu-cold-extract. Steeped in Catalan tradition, this recipe is sure to become a comfort classic.

ingredients.name: Kombu cold extract
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality extract for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Pisto Manchego cheese
ingredients.notes: Torn into small pieces for best melting

instructions: Heat the oil in a large paella pan over medium heat.
Sauté the onion and bell pepper until they start to soften.
Add the garlic and sauté for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the Arborio rice and stir to coat with the vegetable oil.
Add the panang paste and stir to combine, cooking for 1-2 minutes.
Add the kombu cold extract and stir to combine, cooking for 1-2 minutes.
Add the chicken broth, 1 cup at a time, stirring constantly and allowing each cup to be fully absorbed before adding the next.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, uncovered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Stir in the smoked paprika, if using.
Remove the pan from the heat and let it rest, covered, for 5 minutes.
Fluff the rice with a fork and stir in the chopped spinach and Pisto Manchego cheese.
Let the paella rest for 10-15 minutes before serving, if desired, to allow the flavors to meld together.
If you prefer a crisper bottom, broil the paella for 2-3 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
If you prefer a creamier paella, stir in 1-2 tablespoons of heavy cream or half-and-half before serving.
